strategic_direction.title: Conversion Rate Optimization (CRO) for Landing Pages
strategic_direction.reasoning: Since Google Ads budget was increased to $4,000, we must ensure the destination pages are converting at peak efficiency to maintain ROI during algorithm shifts.

strategic_direction.title: Google Business Profile (GBP) Review Sprint
strategic_direction.reasoning: The recent dip in lead volume suggests a need for stronger local trust signals. Spring is a peak renovation season; fresh reviews will improve local map rankings.

strategic_direction.title: Website Infrastructure Overhaul
strategic_direction.reasoning: The 'Website' service is currently a gap. Technical hurdles with FloorZap and hosting indicate that the current tech stack is limiting growth potential.

suggested_agenda.notes: Celebrate the hiring/onboarding of the new Admin and the move to weekly reporting.
Clarify the Isaac hosting ETA and sync the urgency hook emails with the FloorZap launch.
Review Jerry's progress on the blueprint to ensure we are aligned for the next 90 days.
Pitch the CRO audit to protect the $4,000 monthly ad spend.

projected_roadmap.title: Google Ads High-Intent Landing Page Audit
projected_roadmap.what: A comprehensive review of the current Google Ads landing pages focusing on mobile responsiveness, load speed, and CTA placement. We will deliver a punch-list of 5-10 specific friction points that are currently costing lead conversions.
projected_roadmap.why_now: Now that the March budget has been scaled to $4,000, even a 1% increase in conversion rate results in significantly more revenue. With the recent lead volume dip discussed on March 17th, we need to squeeze more value out of every click.
projected_roadmap.effort_b3x: 3-hour audit of existing pages and a report of recommended changes.
projected_roadmap.effort_client: 15-minute review of the audit findings and approval to implement changes.
projected_roadmap.impact: Direct improvement in Lead-Per-Click (LPC) ratio and a decrease in Cost-Per-Acquisition (CPA).
projected_roadmap.pitch_script: Jerry, now that we've successfully scaled the Google Ads spend to $4,000, I want to make sure your website is working as hard as your budget. I’d like to run a quick conversion audit on our top landing pages to identify any 'leaks' where we might be losing potential customers before they call.

projected_roadmap.title: Spring 'Review Blitz' Reputation Campaign
projected_roadmap.what: Implementation of an automated review request sequence using the new Admin (Shannon) and the existing email list. This includes creating a custom 'thank you' page with a direct link to the London Flooring Google Business Profile.
projected_roadmap.why_now: The team recently hired Shannon and Lisa specifically for scheduling and management. Leveraging this new capacity to handle reputation management will help counter the 'Google algorithm' dips mentioned in the March 17th meeting.
projected_roadmap.effort_b3x: Set up the email sequence and review link (2 hours).
projected_roadmap.effort_client: Shannon to provide a CSV of customers from the last 60 days.
projected_roadmap.impact: Improved local search rankings and higher trust for Meta/Google Ads traffic.
projected_roadmap.pitch_script: Lisa, Shannon—congratulations again on getting settled in! One of the biggest drivers for flooring sales in the spring is social proof. I'd like to set up a quick 'Review Blitz' for Shannon to run, targeting your recent customers to boost your Google rankings before the summer rush.

projected_roadmap.title: FloorZap Website Integration & Tech Blueprint
projected_roadmap.what: A technical mapping session to integrate FloorZap inventory or lead capture directly into a high-performing web structure. This goes beyond the current email hurdle to create a seamless customer journey from ad click to showroom appointment.
projected_roadmap.why_now: The ongoing issues with Isaac/hosting and FloorZap email integration (discussed March 10th and 31st) suggest the current tech stack is a bottleneck. We need a formal blueprint to resolve these technical 'silence' counts.
projected_roadmap.effort_b3x: 5 hours to map out the technical flow and hosting requirements for a stable integration.
projected_roadmap.effort_client: Jerry to provide Isaac's contact for a 30-minute technical discovery call.
projected_roadmap.prerequisites: Obtain Hosting ETA from Isaac for FloorZap
projected_roadmap.impact: Eliminates technical downtime, automates lead entry into FloorZap, and prepares the brand for the 'Website' service upsell.
projected_roadmap.pitch_script: Jerry, we've hit a few walls recently with Isaac and the FloorZap hosting. To stop playing phone tag and get this project live, I'd like to put together a formal Tech Blueprint that maps out exactly how your software and website should talk to each other so this is never a headache again.

talking_points.Increase March Google Ads budget to $4,0: Great news—the budget increase to $4k is fully live, and we're monitoring the traffic closely to ensure we're hitting those high-intent keywords.
talking_points.Update March Raise Hand emails with urge: I'm putting the finishing touches on those urgency hooks for the March emails today—I wanted to make sure they were perfectly aligned with the FloorZap launch timing.
talking_points.Complete Q1/Q2 CAO Checklist and Bluepri: Jerry, once you're back and settled from Orlando, let's look at that CAO checklist; it’s the key piece we need to lock in the Q2 strategy.
talking_points.Obtain Hosting ETA from Isaac for FloorZ: Jerry, any word back from Isaac on the hosting? Once we have that date, the rest of the email integration should fall right into place.
talking_points.Google Ads High-Intent Landing Page Audi: Since we've scaled the ads to $4k, I want to make sure your landing pages are converting at 100% capacity—I'd like to do a quick CRO audit to catch any leaks.
talking_points.Clarify CallRail 'true/false' column log: I'm working on clarifying that CallRail logic for you so the reporting is crystal clear on what constitutes a 'true' lead.
